title: Wedding Videography

jokes.text: Yesterday I wrote the word law on the board. This time, don't erase it, and maybe tomorrow we'll get even further.
jokes.setup: Jeff addressing his law class about progress
jokes.punchline: The bar for educational achievement is literally not erasing one word
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Jeff's teaching incompetence - treating not erasing a single word as ambitious progress

jokes.text: By the way, everyone in class gets the credit because I'm not sure how much I should encourage this.
jokes.setup: Garrett is about to give a presentation for extra credit
jokes.punchline: Jeff preemptively gives everyone credit because he's unsure about encouraging student initiative
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Jeff's reluctance to actually teach or evaluate

jokes.text: This is me, Garrett. I'm in your class.
jokes.setup: Garrett introducing himself in a presentation
jokes.punchline: He feels the need to clarify his identity to his own classmates
jokes.explanation: Captures Garrett's social awkwardness and forgettable presence

jokes.text: He said he was there for the such.
jokes.setup: Garrett explaining how he met Stacy at 'Pencils and Such'
jokes.punchline: He was shopping for 'the such' - treating it as an actual product category
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that perfectly captures Garrett's literal-minded weirdness in just four words

jokes.text: I remember I was sweating a lot, and breathing heavy, and my heart felt like it was going to burst in my chest. But the day I met Stacy, most of those symptoms actually declined.
jokes.setup: Garrett describing romantic symptoms when he met Stacy
jokes.punchline: Those were his baseline anxiety symptoms that improved when he met her
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection that reframes romantic clichés as anxiety disorder symptoms

jokes.text: Aren't you still smelling hair to tell Frankie from Annie?
jokes.setup: Abed claiming he's getting better at noticing faces
jokes.punchline: He still uses smell instead of visual recognition to distinguish people
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific joke about Abed's social processing differences

jokes.text: Well, it's official. I'm getting laid.
jokes.setup: Context suggests this is Abed's matter-of-fact response to the wedding situation
jokes.punchline: Abed's clinical announcement of his romantic prospects
jokes.explanation: Classic Abed deadpan delivery of personal information

jokes.text: If you don't put that silly thing away, I swear, I'm gonna stop loving you. Oh, that's it. Happy birthday.
jokes.setup: Annie threatening to stop loving someone if they don't put something away
jokes.punchline: She immediately follows through and switches to generic birthday wishes
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from threat to immediate execution to mundane sign-off

jokes.text: We're making footage like that for me in case I get kidnapped or murdered.
jokes.setup: Explaining they're recreating 'missing lover' footage from movies
jokes.punchline: Annie's matter-of-fact preparation for her own potential demise
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with Annie's characteristic earnestness makes it absurd

jokes.text: The health department called. They don't want anything back.
jokes.setup: Someone commented that their activity is 'super healthy'
jokes.punchline: Britta's nonsensical response about the health department rejecting returns
jokes.explanation: Typical Britta non-sequitur that doesn't quite land but fits her character

jokes.text: For those who don't know, Garrett, he's a guy we don't really know, that we've known for like six years at school, the funny-looking guy. Britta, mean! Sorry! The eccentric-looking guy.
jokes.setup: Britta introducing Garrett to the documentary
jokes.punchline: She catches herself being mean and 'corrects' to equally insulting language
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Britta's attempt at political correctness that misses the mark

jokes.text: But if we're both cleaning then there's nobody to explain why.
jokes.setup: Discussion about what to do for Abed's documentary while cleaning
jokes.punchline: Annie realizes the documentary needs exposition, not just action
jokes.explanation: Nice meta joke about documentary filmmaking conventions

jokes.text: We're an intensely, intimately bonded crew.
jokes.setup: Annie describing their friend group to the documentary
jokes.punchline: The overly dramatic language followed by immediate contradiction from their behavior
jokes.explanation: Sets up the irony that will be contradicted by their subsequent behavior

jokes.text: That beleaguered sigh means, no, I can't have black nails.
jokes.setup: Annie interpreting Britta's sigh about nail polish colors
jokes.punchline: She reads complex emotional subtext into a simple sigh about nail polish
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Annie's over-analytical nature

jokes.text: Please don't Jim the camera like that.
jokes.setup: Someone is looking at the camera in a knowing way
jokes.punchline: Using 'Jim' as a verb referencing The Office's Jim Halpert's camera looks
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-reference turning a character's name into a verb for a specific type of camera look

jokes.text: Your what?! Don't jump on that, my accountant, it's a figure of speech. It's a form of speech called making things up.
jokes.setup: Britta mentions her accountant, others react with surprise
jokes.punchline: She admits she made it up and calls it a 'figure of speech' then defines it as lying
jokes.explanation: Perfect Britta moment - getting caught in a lie and trying to intellectualize her way out

jokes.text: A cold, off-putting incompatible person. The kind of person that turns a sleepover into a stoning.
jokes.setup: Frankie explaining what kind of person says no to getting ready together
jokes.punchline: Escalating from social awkwardness to biblical violence
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from mild social rejection to extreme biblical punishment

jokes.text: Biblical, igneous, jagged, hateful, pointy, ninja rocks
jokes.setup: Frankie describing the rocks thrown at her
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and absurd adjectives ending with 'ninja rocks'
jokes.explanation: Good escalating adjective list with mixing geological terms and 'ninja rocks'

jokes.text: One of my sisters as well, you know, one of them is deceased, and the other is mentally retarded.
jokes.setup: Casual conversation about personal affairs
jokes.punchline: Frankie drops devastating personal information inappropriately casually
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable humor from inappropriate oversharing, though the language is dated

jokes.text: We batted the time. Huh? We lied to you cause we knew you'd be late. So we lied to you about the time.
jokes.setup: Explaining why they're not in a hurry
jokes.punchline: Chang invents 'batted the time' then has to explain the deception twice
jokes.explanation: Typical Chang malapropism and over-explanation

jokes.text: You need people whose flaws feed into each other. It's what do you call it? Co-dependence. Synergy.
jokes.setup: Chang philosophizing about team dynamics
jokes.punchline: Annie correctly identifies it as codependence, Chang 'corrects' to business jargon
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of corporate speak masking dysfunctional relationships

jokes.text: You literally just defined co-dependence.
jokes.setup: Chang's 'synergy' explanation
jokes.punchline: Britta points out he's describing exactly what Annie said
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response calling out the circular logic

jokes.text: We invented a new game. Celebrity Garrett marriage.
jokes.setup: Context shows they're playing a wedding ceremony game
jokes.punchline: They've created a game of marrying Garrett to celebrities
jokes.explanation: Absurd party game concept that's both creative and slightly cruel to Garrett

jokes.text: We were genius to pad the time margin. Because now we have, like, three more hours to play the game.
jokes.setup: Chang celebrating their time management
jokes.punchline: He reveals they have three MORE hours, meaning they're actually very late
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that reveals their time calculation was completely wrong

jokes.text: I'm home alone. Well, not really.
jokes.setup: Chang saying he's been left home alone
jokes.punchline: He immediately contradicts himself, presumably because Abed is filming him
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag about forgetting the cameraman is present

jokes.text: What if I'm God?
jokes.setup: Officiant philosophizing about choosing belief systems during ceremony
jokes.punchline: Casually suggests he might be God himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of new-age wedding officiant rambling to ultimate megalomania

jokes.text: I know I look pretty complete. Somebody over there laughed at that the wrong way.
jokes.setup: Garrett saying he's incomplete without Stacy but looks complete
jokes.punchline: He breaks character to address someone laughing inappropriately at his appearance
jokes.explanation: Great meta moment where character acknowledges audience reaction to his looks mid-ceremony

jokes.text: Your was louder than what I said.
jokes.setup: Study group reacting loudly to Garrett's vows
jokes.punchline: Garrett pointing out their reaction was louder than his actual vow
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment highlighting how the group disrupts the ceremony

jokes.text: Well that, those aren't really vows. That's a list of things you love about me, yeah.
jokes.setup: Stacy finishing what she thought were her vows
jokes.punchline: Garrett matter-of-factly corrects her understanding of what vows are
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment showing the couple's different understanding of their own ceremony

jokes.text: Definitely not Garrett, since his name's not Barrett. Whoopsie. We say a wrong a name. Oh! Racist. Oh, no. You a racist. That guy's a racist. From here to there is racist.
jokes.setup: Someone calls Garrett 'Barrett' incorrectly
jokes.punchline: Chang escalates from name correction to calling everyone racist
jokes.explanation: Typical Chang escalation from minor mistake to dramatic accusations

jokes.text: You came to a wedding in the middle of the vows through a bush. I'm not Dr. Phil, but, I don't want my son to kill himself, so, do what you gotta do!
jokes.setup: Garrett's mom addressing the study group's disruptive behavior
jokes.punchline: Escalates from reasonable criticism to suicide concern with cheerful delivery
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from social criticism to dark parental concern delivered with inappropriate cheer

jokes.text: It's almost like groups and people in groups, can never be complete at the same time. Does that make any sense? No? Well, that's your problem.
jokes.setup: Abed making a philosophical observation about group dynamics
jokes.punchline: He dismisses the audience's comprehension as their problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed moment - profound observation followed by dismissive attitude

jokes.text: My name is Elroy Patashnik, and from 2006 to 2009, I was addicted to encouraging white people.
jokes.setup: Elroy introducing himself in documentary style
jokes.punchline: Treating encouragement like a clinical addiction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant social commentary framed as addiction recovery - original, economical, and perfectly captures workplace dynamics

jokes.text: My name is Elroy Patashnik, and from 2006 to 2009, I was addicted to encouraging white people.
jokes.setup: Elroy introducing himself with what sounds like a confession
jokes.punchline: Reveals his 'addiction' is to encouraging white people
jokes.explanation: Brilliant setup treating racial workplace dynamics as an addiction with recovery meeting format

jokes.text: The thing is, and this will sound racist, white people are very discouraged, and very discouraging to each other.
jokes.setup: Elroy prefacing an observation about race
jokes.punchline: His 'racist' observation is actually a supportive psychological insight
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of 'this will sound racist' setup with actually insightful social observation

jokes.text: There's no lid.
jokes.setup: Elroy's previous explanation about putting a lid on his encouraging addiction
jokes.punchline: He realizes he's relapsed and there's no stopping it
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to his earlier metaphor about addiction, delivered with dawning horror

jokes.text: Am I that bad? We have the same dragon. Eventually you will slay it, or train it, or dissolve in its stomach. Its name is Helping Others.
jokes.setup: Annie asking if her helpfulness is problematic
jokes.punchline: Abed frames it as a shared dragon with multiple possible outcomes
jokes.explanation: Beautiful extended metaphor turning character flaws into fantasy RPG elements

jokes.text: If you slay it, I don't know. I guess you get a new dragon named Yourself. And then you'd be Jeff Winger.
jokes.setup: Continuing the dragon metaphor about slaying vs training
jokes.punchline: Getting rid of helping others makes you selfish like Jeff
jokes.explanation: Perfect character insight wrapped in fantasy metaphor - Jeff as the endpoint of selfish evolution

jokes.text: Carson Scaley. Jude Claw. Ariana Puffington. I got into a bit of a celebrity pun barrel here.
jokes.setup: Listing dragon names that are celebrity puns
jokes.punchline: His self-aware acknowledgment of getting carried away with puns
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor about getting caught up in wordplay, though the puns themselves are fairly standard

jokes.text: Where's my brother!? I've gotta thing to say about my friend Garrett!
jokes.setup: Garrett's brother Bones arriving at wedding
jokes.punchline: His drunken, aggressive entrance disrupting the reception
jokes.explanation: Standard drunk relative at wedding trope, though serves story function

jokes.text: That was Stacy's first time! But it's not gonna be her last time, right Garrett?
jokes.setup: Someone giving a wedding toast
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual reference at a wedding
jokes.explanation: Basic inappropriate wedding toast humor - effective cringe but not particularly clever

jokes.text: A fire hydrant could follow this. Don't let Garrett keep you from painting. Don't paint.
jokes.setup: Jeff critiquing the previous toast's quality
jokes.punchline: Claims even a fire hydrant could give a better toast, then demonstrates equally bad advice
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Jeff's confidence followed by equally poor execution

jokes.text: like all my students, I give them till about June.
jokes.setup: Jeff talking about the couple in his toast
jokes.punchline: Predicts their marriage will fail by June like his students
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff moment - inappropriately honest and connecting teaching failure to marriage failure

jokes.text: Or go near a child. Or be in the same town as a pair of scissors. This girl's a wreck.
jokes.setup: Jeff insulting Andi in his toast
jokes.punchline: Escalating restrictions on her behavior ending with brutal assessment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of insults with increasingly specific restrictions

jokes.text: either someone here is lying, or this family's a lot closer than we thought.
jokes.setup: Jeff realizes the two 90-year-old women are the same person
jokes.punchline: Euphemistically suggests the family is 'closer' (implying incest) than expected
jokes.explanation: Perfect euphemistic setup for the incest revelation - masterfully understated

jokes.text: And now, this is a man that knows how to marry his cousin.
jokes.setup: Revelation that Garrett married his cousin
jokes.punchline: Elroy's encouraging addiction leads him to praise even incest
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Elroy's addiction is so strong he encourages even incest

jokes.text: None of us would have met if Hitler hadn't been born. Also none of Britta's arguments would have a default analogy.
jokes.setup: Discussion about regret and causality
jokes.punchline: Abed points out Britta's reliance on Hitler comparisons
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on Britta's argumentative patterns disguised as philosophical observation

jokes.text: What are you, Hitler, Hitler?
jokes.setup: Abed calling out her Hitler references
jokes.punchline: Britta immediately proves his point by calling him Hitler
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate proof of Abed's point - Britta reflexively goes to Hitler analogy

jokes.text: Garrett, who loves you here more than Stacy? Nobody. Stacy, is it your fault Garrett's your cousin? No.
jokes.setup: Chang defending the couple's right to stay married
jokes.punchline: His logic questions focus on love and fault regarding incest
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic applying relationship advice to incest situation

jokes.text: Stacy, will you be my legally incestuous wife?
jokes.setup: Chang's speech convincing them to stay together
jokes.punchline: Garrett embraces the situation with formal proposal language
jokes.explanation: Perfect formal proposal language applied to taboo situation - absurd but committed

jokes.text: Everyone stay and eat cake, or go to hell.
jokes.setup: Garrett asserting control over his wedding
jokes.punchline: Aggressive hospitality with hell as the alternative to cake
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of wedding hosting duties to ultimatum level

jokes.text: Did you know that first cousins can have children without great risk of birth defect or genetic disease? That's a quote directly from the New York Times, April 4, 2002.
jokes.setup: Writer appearing to discuss the episode's theme
jokes.punchline: Cites oddly specific newspaper quote defending cousin marriage
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke with writer 'defending' his episode choice with suspiciously specific research

jokes.text: I can make love to my cousin in Nebraska, but if I take her on a date in South Dakota, I'm looking at 15 years prison time.
jokes.setup: Writer discussing inconsistent incest laws
jokes.punchline: Personal example of interstate incest law complications
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation making the 'educational' segment disturbingly personal

tmdb_overview: When two Greendale students get married, the wedding reception takes a turn after Jeff unwittingly exposes an inconvenient truth.
insight: Season 6's tonal pivot lands: character comedy sustains 85-point episode on escalation alone.
